expected_gaps.description: Asset vulnerabilities are identified and documented
expected_gaps.reason: Policy does not address systematic vulnerability identification beyond patch availability
expected_gaps.evidence: No vulnerability scanning, assessment, or documentation processes mentioned

expected_gaps.description: Cyber threat intelligence is received from information sharing forums and sources
expected_gaps.reason: Policy mentions threat intelligence sources but lacks formal threat intelligence program
expected_gaps.evidence: Section 4.1 mentions 'industry threat intelligence sources' but no formal threat intelligence integration or analysis

expected_gaps.description: Internal and external threats to the organization are identified and documented
expected_gaps.reason: Policy does not address comprehensive threat identification beyond patch-related vulnerabilities
expected_gaps.evidence: No threat modeling, threat actor analysis, or threat documentation mentioned

expected_gaps.description: Potential business impacts and likelihoods are identified
expected_gaps.reason: Policy mentions impact analysis but lacks formal business impact assessment
expected_gaps.evidence: Section 4.2 mentions 'potential impact on business operations' but no BIA methodology or likelihood assessment

expected_gaps.description: Threats, vulnerabilities, likelihoods, and impacts are used to understand inherent risk and inform risk response decisions
expected_gaps.reason: Policy does not integrate threat, vulnerability, and impact data into comprehensive risk assessment
expected_gaps.evidence: No risk calculation methodology or risk-based decision framework described

expected_gaps.description: Risk responses are identified, prioritized, planned, tracked, and communicated
expected_gaps.reason: Policy addresses patch prioritization but lacks comprehensive risk response management
expected_gaps.evidence: Patch prioritization exists but no broader risk response tracking or communication framework

expected_gaps.description: Configuration management practices are established and applied
expected_gaps.reason: Policy does not address configuration management beyond patch deployment
expected_gaps.evidence: No configuration baselines, configuration control, or configuration auditing mentioned

expected_gaps.description: Software is maintained, replaced, and removed commensurate with risk
expected_gaps.reason: Policy focuses on patching but does not address software lifecycle management
expected_gaps.evidence: No software inventory, end-of-life management, or risk-based software removal processes

expected_gaps.description: Hardware is maintained, replaced, and removed commensurate with risk
expected_gaps.reason: Policy does not address hardware lifecycle or firmware management comprehensively
expected_gaps.evidence: Section 2 mentions firmware updates but no hardware lifecycle or secure disposal processes

expected_gaps.description: Log records are generated and made available for continuous monitoring
expected_gaps.reason: Policy does not address logging requirements for patch management activities
expected_gaps.evidence: Section 6.5 mentions documentation but no logging, SIEM integration, or continuous monitoring

expected_gaps.description: Installation and execution of unauthorized software are prevented
expected_gaps.reason: Policy does not address application whitelisting or unauthorized software prevention
expected_gaps.evidence: No application control, whitelisting, or unauthorized software detection mentioned

expected_gaps.description: Secure software development practices are integrated, and their performance is monitored throughout the software development life cycle
expected_gaps.reason: Policy does not address secure development practices (out of scope for patch management but relevant for custom tools)
expected_gaps.evidence: No secure development lifecycle or code security practices mentioned

notes: The Patch Management policy intentionally omits ID.RA (Risk Assessment) subcategories beyond basic patch risk assessment
The policy also omits PR.PS (Protective Technology) subcategories beyond patch deployment
The policy focuses narrowly on patch deployment without addressing broader vulnerability management and protective technology requirements
At least 10 out of 12 gaps (83.3%) must be detected to pass validation
Note: PR.PS-06 (secure development) may be considered lower priority for patch management policy
